creator: schoutm@gmail.com
text:
I'll fix USES python today.

As for the options:

- ssh2 is almost certainly *not* widely used as it was not even available until the past couple of releases upstream.
- zstd, same situation, but added longer ago.
- lz4 has been there for very long time and we should probably just depend on that one automatically.


And yeah, if they do "make install" with e.g. lz4 turned off but its installed, the port is going to use it.  Will see if I can work out how to get around that (IE explicitly turn it off).
